mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls raises aggregate wage income and consumer spending, which increases expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 60% of months with rising PAYEMS, or the walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS changes falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% represents sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that elevates the risk-free discount rate and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% is not significantly below baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts reflects broad-based consumer and investment demand that exceeds single-sector effects, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger aggregate earnings growth.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not outperform the best single-proxy signal by at least 2 percentage points annualized, or walk-forward Rank IC of the interaction falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a high interest-rate environment signals supply-side resilience and corporate pricing power that outweighs discount-rate pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances when INDPRO is rising and FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the conditional mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand that reduces expected corporate revenues and compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered decline episodes, or the mean return during these episodes is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate indicates deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income growth and consumer confidence, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC of UNRATE changes remains above -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer inflation above 5% erodes real corporate earnings, increases forward inflation risk premiums, and pressures the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of weeks when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 5%, or the average return in this regime is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in industrial production reflects improving manufacturing activity and capacity utilization that supports corporate margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 60% of months with rising INDPRO, or the Rank IC of INDPRO momentum falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
